2604.07285.title: Why teaching resists automation in an AI-inundated era: Human judgment, non-modular work, and the limits of delegation
2604.07285.paper_summary: Debates about artificial intelligence (AI) in education often portray teaching as a modular and procedural job that can increasingly be automated or delegated to technology. This brief communication paper argues that such claims depend on treating teaching as more separable than it is in practice. Drawing on recent literature and empirical studies of large language models and retrieval-augmented generation systems, I argue that although AI can support some bounded functions, instructional work remains difficult to automate in meaningful ways because it is inherently interpretive, relational, and grounded in professional judgment. More fundamentally, teaching and learning are shaped by human cognition, behavior, motivation, and social interaction in ways that cannot be fully specified, predicted, or exhaustively modeled. Tasks that may appear separable in principle derive their instructional value in practice from ongoing contextual interpretation across learners, situations, and relationships. As long as educational practice relies on emergent understanding of human cognition and learning, teaching remains a form of professional work that resists automation. AI may improve access to information and support selected instructional activities, but it does not remove the need for human judgment and relational accountability that effective teaching requires.
2604.07285.paper_summary_zh: 教育中關於人工智慧（AI）的辯論常常將教學描繪為一種模組化和程序化的工作，這種工作可以越來越多地被自動化或委託給技術。這篇簡短的交流論文主張，這種說法依賴於將教學視為比實際上更可分離的。根據最近的文獻和對大型語言模型及檢索增強生成系統的實證研究，我主張儘管AI可以支持一些有限的功能，但教學工作仍然難以以有意義的方式自動化，因為它本質上是解釋性的、關係性的，並且根植於專業判斷中。更根本地，教學和學習受到人類認知、行為、動機和社會互動的影響，這些影響無法完全被具體化、預測或徹底建模。那些在原則上看似可分離的任務，實際上其教學價值來自於學習者、情境和關係之間持續的背景解釋。只要教育實踐依賴於對人類認知和學習的突現理解，教學就仍然是一種抵抗自動化的專業工作。AI可能改善對信息的獲取並支持某些選定的教學活動，但它並不消除有效教學所需的人類判斷和關係責任。
2604.07285.author: Songhee Han et.al.
